content.text:
Objectifs de l’enseignement
This course is intended for students pursuing a career in fields related to cyber-security. Since cryptography is present in everyday life (using smartphones, sending messages, paying with a credit card, ....), this course will cover classical cryptography algorithms, Symmetric and asymmetric cryptography, hash functions, digital signatures and other fundamental cryptographic protocols.
Contenu de la matière
Introduction and terminology
Mathematical background
a. GCD, Modulo, Modular inverse,
Cryptographic protocols
Symmetric cryptography:
Cesar, Vigenere, Substitution, Hill, Affine, ...
Public-key cryptography: RSA, Elgamal, ...
Hybrid cryptography
Hash functions:
MD4, MD5, SHA-1, ...
Cryptanalyse: Brute force attack, Frequency analysis
Key exchange Algorithms a. Diffie-Hellman
b. Shamir’s Three-pass protocol
Digital signature
BlockChain and cryptography
Mode d’évaluation : Contrôle continu, examen
Références
”Handbook of applied cryptography”. Menezes Alfred J., Paul C. Van Oorschot and Scott A. Vanstone. CRC press, 2018.
”Applied cryptography: protocols, algorithms, and source code in C”. Schneier Bruce. 1996.
